title: File
description: File wraps the shared field foundation around native file selection with inline and drag-and-drop area modes, built-in file lists, accept filters, and simple limits for count and size.
intro: Use `ui-file` when users need to attach documents, images, or bundles without building a custom upload shell. It supports compact inline selection, larger drop zones, selected-file lists, multiple uploads, and shared field inputs like label, help text, size, variant, readonly, disabled, and validation messaging.

featureSections.title: Basic upload patterns
featureSections.description: Start with a clear label and choose the surface size based on importance. Area mode works for primary upload steps, while inline mode fits compact forms and smaller edits.

featureSections.title: Layout, size, and label position
featureSections.description: The component inherits shared field density and label placement, so it can live comfortably in dense tables, sidebar forms, or roomier task panels without a separate upload implementation.

featureSections.title: Accept filters and limits
featureSections.description: Use `accept`, `maxSize`, and `maxFiles` to narrow what the user can add before your backend workflow starts. This keeps the upload affordance aligned with real product constraints.

featureSections.title: Multiple files and built-in list
featureSections.description: When upload is a bundle instead of a single asset, let the component manage the visible file list and removal actions rather than building separate list UI beside the field.

featureSections.title: Validation, readonly, and disabled
featureSections.description: Required uploads should validate like any other field. Readonly keeps an already attached file visible, while disabled communicates that the upload step is unavailable.

featureSections.title: Reactive form integration
featureSections.description: A practical pattern for settings or creation flows: combine a single-file field with a multi-file field inside one form group, then keep summary actions and current values nearby.

featureSections.title: Document request panel
featureSections.description: A stronger product-facing example: one panel that collects the main delivery files, an optional note, and direct actions to send or save the package.

accessibility.title: Label and description
accessibility.body: Prefer a visible `label` so the upload purpose is explicit. Help and error text are linked through `aria-describedby`, and `ariaLabel` can provide the trigger name when there is no visible label.

accessibility.title: Trigger semantics
accessibility.body: Both modes are driven by a native file input that stays out of the tab order while the visible trigger exposes button-like interaction. Inline mode uses a readonly text field with `role="button"`; area mode uses the drop zone surface with `role="button"`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab | Moves focus to the visible file trigger and, when files are present in area mode, to remove actions. |
| Enter / Space | Opens the native file picker from the visible trigger. |
| Shift+Tab | Returns focus to the previous control in the form flow. |

accessibility.title: File list actions
accessibility.body: When area mode shows selected files, each remove button gets a file-specific accessible name such as `Remove contract.pdf`. The hidden native input remains `tabindex="-1"`, so users do not encounter duplicate focus stops.

apiSections.title: FileComponent - ui-file
apiSections.lead: Standalone file-selection field built on the shared field base. It supports both compact inline selection and larger drop-zone presentation without changing how the rest of your form is wired.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Upload surface style. `inline` behaves like a compact picker; `area` renders a drag-and-drop zone.
Comma-separated native file filters such as `image/*` or `.pdf,.docx`.
Allows more than one selected file.
Caps how many files can be added when `multiple` is enabled.
'Click to upload or drag and drop'
Primary message inside area mode when no file is selected.
Secondary guidance below the main upload message.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits after new files are accepted by filters and limits.
Emits when a selected file is removed or cleared.
Single file for default mode, file array for `multiple`, or `null` when empty.

paragraphs: The component extends the shared `FieldComponent`, so help text, error handling, CVA integration, and described-by wiring follow the same model as the rest of the field family.
Area mode includes a built-in selected-file list with remove actions, which is usually preferable to composing a separate preview list for standard document upload flows.
blocks.title: Notes
